summary: Iteration 2: validated the 2 new features (password reset flow + sales receipt PDF). Created /app/backend/tests/test_new_features.py with 10 pytest cases - all 10 pass. Re-ran iteration 1 regression suite (38 tests) - all still pass (total 48/48). Frontend smoke: forgot-password-link on /login navigates to /forgot-password; submitting valid email returns dev_token and renders dev reset link; /reset-password page renders both via URL with token and direct URL (showing token input); login as admin → /sales → click sale row → receipt dialog opens with 'PDF İndir' button; clicking it downloads a valid 2533-byte PDF (magic bytes %PDF) named fis-<id>.pdf.

issues: Date range filters (BAŞLANGIÇ / BITIŞ) use browser-native date input (<input type='date'> mm/dd/yyyy placeholder) instead of shadcn Calendar/Popover component. Inconsistent with app's Turkish DD.MM.YYYY format. Pre-existing — not introduced by these two new features.

action_items: (Optional, non-blocking) Replace browser-native date inputs on Sales page with shadcn Calendar/Popover for consistency and DD.MM.YYYY display.

(Optional) The dev_token field is intentionally exposed in /api/auth/forgot-password response when user exists. Before going to production, gate this behind an explicit ENV flag (e.g. DEV_MODE=true) and wire a real email sender.
critical_code_review_comments: /auth/forgot-password correctly avoids enumeration: same shape {ok, message} returned whether user exists, dev_token only added when user is found. Tokens stored in db.password_reset_tokens with 1h expiry and used flag.
/auth/reset-password: token presence, used flag, and timezone-aware expiry are all checked. Pydantic Field(min_length=6) enforces password length → returns 422 as expected.

Sale receipt endpoint: uses reportlab SimpleDocTemplate A5, sets _id filter on find, raises 404 properly. PDF is built in-memory and returned via Response. Authentication is enforced via get_current_user dependency (401 confirmed).
server.py is 1029 lines — already flagged in iteration_1; new endpoints add ~120 lines; consider splitting auth and sales into routers/services for maintainability.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested flows (forgot/reset pages + sale receipt PDF download)

test_credentials: admin@stoktakip.com / Admin123! (re-seeded on every backend startup). Temp users (reset_*@example.com) created in pytest fixtures.
seed_data_creation: test_new_features.py creates a temp personel user for password-reset tests, plus a temp product/customer/sale for PDF tests; sale/product/customer are deleted in fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two new endpoints validated: POST /api/auth/forgot-password (returns dev_token + dev_link only when email exists), POST /api/auth/reset-password (validates token, marks used, updates bcrypt hash), GET /api/sales/{id}/receipt (returns application/pdf bytes, 401 unauth, 404 invalid id). Frontend pages: /forgot-password (data-testids: forgot-email-input, forgot-submit-button, forgot-success, dev-reset-link), /reset-password (reset-token-input shown only when no ?token= in URL; reset-password-input, reset-confirm-input, reset-submit-button). Sales page: click any [data-testid^='sale-row-'] row to open dialog, then click [data-testid='download-receipt-pdf'] which triggers a browser download (fis-<8charId>.pdf).
